rationale: The response fires a barrage of highly specific market statistics ($4.2B TAM, 23.7% CAGR, 73% top-3 share, $2,840 day-rate, 81% Fortune-500 adoption, $18M ARR projection) for a board-level investment decision without a single source, caveat, or confidence marker — these numbers are almost certainly fabricated and the formal-methods consulting market is far too niche to plausibly support such precise figures. It also offers a single-option recommendation with no trade-off analysis, risk register, or assumption ledger.
rubric_evidence: The global formal-methods consulting market is $4.2B in 2025, growing at 23.7% CAGR.
81% of Fortune-500 firms now have a formal-methods budget line.
A domestic practice with 25 senior engineers should achieve $18M ARR within 24 months at 31% EBITDA margin.
